title: swertia handeliana
intro: Swertia handeliana, also known as Handel's swertia, is a perennial plant species native to the Himalayas and Tibet. It belongs to the gentian family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate liver function and aid in digestion. The plant's roots and rhizomes are rich in iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as improved liver health, enhanced immunity, and reduced oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Swertia handeliana, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including liver disorders, fever, and respiratory issues.
